title: Gen Z Hates Marriage and Kids... Or Do They?! | Institute for Family Studies
gist: IFS teaser for an Isabel Brown Show episode where sociologist Brad Wilcox, author of 'Get Married,' argues a dominant cultural narrative wrongly frames marriage and kids as life-ending, driving Gen Z's anti-marriage, anti-fertility turn amid record-low US marriage and fertility rates.

insights.claim: The United States is simultaneously experiencing a record-low marriage rate and an all-time-low fertility rate, prompting scholars to ask how the trend arose and how to reverse it.
insights.speaker: Institute for Family Studies
insights.so_what: Confirms the marriage and fertility declines are compounding and simultaneous rather than independent trends, reinforcing long-run demographic headwinds for household formation and consumption.
insights.quote: the United States is living through a record low marriage rate and an all time low fertility rate
insights.locator: As a result, the United States is living through

insights.claim: The episode argues a dominant cultural narrative tells young people their lives are effectively over the moment they marry or have children, and frames this narrative as a key driver of Gen Z's anti-marriage, anti-kids sentiment.
insights.speaker: Institute for Family Studies
insights.so_what: If narrative/media framing rather than economics is a primary driver, messaging and cultural interventions -- not just financial policy -- could move family-formation trends.
insights.quote: the attack on marriage and having kids, telling us that our lives are effectively OVER the moment we say "I do" or bring new life into the world
insights.locator: Nothing has been a more powerful cultural narrative

insights.claim: Brad Wilcox's book 'Get Married: Why Americans Must Defy the Elites, Forge Strong Families, and Save Civilization' frames reversing the marriage decline as requiring Americans to actively reject elite cultural narratives.
insights.speaker: Institute for Family Studies
insights.so_what: Signals a broader elite-vs-populist framing being applied to family policy discourse, relevant to how pro-natalist messaging is likely to be pitched going forward.
insights.locator: author of "Get Married: Why Americans Must Defy the Elites

insights.claim: The episode is positioned as diagnosing why Gen Z is trending anti-marriage and anti-kids and what culture can do to reverse it, though no specific remedies are detailed in this teaser.
insights.speaker: Institute for Family Studies
insights.so_what: Flags an open, unresolved policy/culture question worth monitoring in follow-on IFS content rather than a settled thesis.
insights.locator: getting to the bottom of why Gen Z has been trending anti-marriage
